chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 1799 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders as to maintainability of petition. 14.05.2025 Mr. Muhammad Saleem is present on behalf of petitioner. On the last date, following office objections were recorded and petitioner was given a further opportunity to ad8dress the same: “1. The claim of Petitioner is that the Petitioner is elected President of Trade Union and his name appeared in ballot for Hajj and Usually Sindh Worker Board sent 10 persons for Hajj but no authentic document for said Scheme was filed to show the legality of said Scheme under the relevant law, so how this Petition is maintainable ? 2. Which law and rule of Worker Welfare Ordinance 1971 Authorize the authorities to distribute the amount of thousands of workers without their consent to utilities for Hajj of Others? 3. Why Selected Workers are not filing Petition. 4. The Resolution (Annexure P/2) Page No. 15 is on Colour Print Paper, so Proper Resolution of Petitioner to be filed. 5. Prayer clause to be specified with regard to WWF Islamabad. 6. Legible copy of Page No. 19 to be filed. The President of Petitioner’s Union Muhammad Saleem is directed to satisfy as to maintainability. To come up on 14.05.2025.” Today, once again he remains unable to satisfy the court on the count of maintainability. Petition is found to be mis-conceived, hence, dismissed in limine. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
